event: no history of drug addiction or intoxication
provisional diagnosis of acute febrile illness with undifferentiated fever
workup to rule out tropical infections
scrub typhus antigen card was positive
confirmed the diagnosis of scrub typhus
blood and other body fluid cultures were sterile
liver function test showed mild hyperbilirubinemia with transaminitis
negative for hepatitis B antigen and hepatitis C antibody
ultrasound of the abdomen showed mild fatty infiltration of the liver
treated with tablet doxycycline 100 mg twice daily
defervescence occurred in the next 2 days
developed weakness of both lower limbs (Grade 2/5)
shifted to the Intensive Care Unit (ICU)
single breath count was 10 with respiratory acidosis
intubated and put on mechanical ventilation
Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the brain was done
MRI of the cervical spine was done
nerve conduction velocity (NCV) showed motor sensory demyelinating polyneuropathy
cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis did not reveal albuminocytologic dissociation
started on intravenous immunoglobulin therapy 0.4 mg/kg/day for 5 days
Tablet rifampicin 600 mg twice daily for meningoencephalitis was added
showed improvement in weakness of all four limbs
gradually weaned off and extubated from the ventilator
aggressive limb and chest physiotherapy was continued
discharged from the hospital with full neurological recovery
regularly followed up in the outpatient department
